title: Savory Sweet Peruvian Peppers Rocoto Relleno
description: A fresh and juicy Peruvian dish featuring Thai chilli jam, fried to perfection in a cataplana vessel, kosher friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cut off the tops of the Rocoto peppers and remove the seeds and membranes.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium high heat and fry the peppers until charred.
Remove the peppers from the skillet and set aside to cool.
In the same skillet, add the chopped onion and cook until translucent.
Add the garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the ground beef and cook until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Stir in the Thai chilli jam, cilantro, lime juice, salt, and black pepper.
Stuff each pepper with the beef mixture and place in a cataplana vessel.
Cover the vessel and bake for 25 minutes.
Serve the peppers hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
